senses.definition: from the beginning or continuously throughout a period of time

examples.x: She knew the answer all along but chose not to reveal it until now.
They were friends all along, despite occasional disagreements.
The solution to the problem was evident all along.
He had the key to the puzzle all along.
He was aware of the mistake all along but didn't point it out.

senses.definition: used to express that a condition must be met for something to happen or be true
used to indicate that something is happening or being done in the usual or customary way

examples.x: As per usual, he arrived at the office early to start his work.
As per usual, she forgot to bring her umbrella on a rainy day.
As per usual, the teacher assigned homework for the weekend.
He arrived late for the meeting, as per usual.
As per usual, he took his morning walk in the park.
The decision remains undecided as yet.
As yet, no one has claimed responsibility for the incident.
The details of the project are incomplete as yet.
The solution to the problem has not been found as yet.
The investigation has as yet not uncovered any new evidence.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something will happen or be accomplished eventually or after a period of time

examples.x: We'll complete the project, and by and by, we'll see the results of our hard work.
By and by, you'll learn to play the piano with practice.
With dedication and effort, success will come by and by.
By and by, you'll understand the complexities of this subject.
By and by, you'll recover from your illness and feel better.

senses.definition: used to refer to a point in the future or a specific moment that is coming soon

examples.x: We have a meeting scheduled up ahead in the afternoon.
There's a deadline up ahead, so we need to finish the project quickly.
Keep working hard; success is up ahead if you stay focused.
There's a busy period up ahead, so we need to prepare for the increased workload.
Let's keep saving money; there may be unexpected expenses up ahead.

senses.definition: at the current moment or during the existing time

examples.x: The store is closed at present for inventory management.
At present, we are not accepting new applications for the program.
The website is experiencing technical issues at present; please try again later.
At present, the city is undergoing significant infrastructure improvements.
The product is not available at present, but it will be restocked next week.

senses.definition: for a limited period, usually until a certain condition changes

examples.x: For the time being, I am focusing on my studies before deciding on my career path.
She is serving as the interim manager for the time being until a permanent replacement is found.
We'll use this office for the time being until our new space is ready.
I'll take care of this task for the time being, but we should discuss a more permanent solution.
The current arrangement is acceptable for the time being, but we'll need a long-term plan.

senses.definition: at the appropriate or expected time, without rushing or delay

examples.x: The details of the project will be revealed to the team in due course.
Please be patient; your request will be processed in due time.
The project will be completed in due course, according to the schedule.
You will receive a response to your inquiry in due time.
The product will be available for purchase in due course; please check back later.

senses.definition: used to explain the main reason or starting point of a situation

examples.x: I don't want to go in the first place, I'm tired, and secondly, I can't afford it.
In the first place, the data is flawed, which invalidates the entire study.
In the first place, we don't have enough funding for this project, and secondly, we're short-staffed.
In the first place, this project was poorly planned, so failure was inevitable.

senses.definition: only a few moments before it is still possible to get something done or avoid something bad from happening

examples.x: He arrives in the nick of time to catch his flight before the gate closes.
The firefighter rescues the trapped cat from the burning building in the nick of time.
They submit their project proposal just in the nick of time, minutes before the deadline.
She called me in the nick of time, before I started worrying.
In the nick of time, we managed to catch the last train.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something will happen immediately after a certain condition or event occurs

examples.x: I'll call you as soon as I finish work.
She'll email you the document as soon as she receives it.
We'll leave as soon as the rain stops.
As soon as the clock strikes noon, we'll have lunch.
They'll begin the presentation as soon as the projector is set up.

senses.definition: used to emphasize the present moment in relation to the topic under discussion

examples.x: At this point in time, we don't have enough information to make a decision.
I cannot provide an update on the project at this point in time.
At this point in time, we are still assessing the situation.
We cannot predict the outcome at this point in time.
We're focusing on addressing immediate concerns at this point in time.

senses.definition: used to emphasize a specific moment or period in history or a past event

examples.x: At that point in time, the country was experiencing economic prosperity.
The decision seemed reasonable at that point in time, given the information available.
At that point in time, technology had not yet advanced to where it is today.
He was the leader of the company at that point in time.
She made the best decision she could at that point in time.

senses.definition: exactly at the specified time, neither late nor early

examples.x: With patience and dedication, you will succeed in time.
The flowers will bloom in time, just be patient.
He knew that, in time, he would learn to play the piano well.
In time, you'll come to realize the value of hard work and perseverance.
He was confused at first, but he understood the concept in time.

senses.definition: in a short amount of time

examples.x: I'll be with you in a tick; just need to finish this quick task.
Hold on, I'll grab your jacket in a tick before we leave.
Dinner will be ready in a tick, so please set the table.
I'll answer your question in a tick; let me check my notes first.
Just wait in a tick, and I'll be back with the information you need.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something is not expected to happen in the near future or immediately

examples.x: I don't think we'll be getting a response from them anytime soon; they seem to be very busy.
Don't expect the construction to be finished anytime soon; it's a long-term project.
Unfortunately, it doesn't look like the weather will improve anytime soon; we might need to cancel our outdoor plans.
I don't see him changing his mind about that decision anytime soon; he's pretty stubborn.
With the current situation, it's unlikely that we'll be able to travel internationally anytime soon.
